reason: `retry=True` maps to uncapped `RetryStrategy()`, while `retry=<int>` sets `max_attempts`; cutoff check is `job.attempts >= max_attempts`, so 5 retries means up to 6 executions.
`RetryStrategy` docstring says max_attempts is retries, and code uses attempts starting at 0 on first run, so `max_attempts=3` allows four total executions.
`RetryStrategy` defines `wait`, `linear_wait`, `exponential_wait`, and `retry_exceptions` for exception-type filtering (implemented as iterable of exception classes; sets are valid inputs).
Custom strategies override `get_retry_decision()` returning `None` or `RetryDecision`; `RetryDecision` supports `retry_in`/`retry_at` and optional `priority`/`queue`/`lock` overrides.
Job datetime validator raises `ValueError("Timezone aware datetime is required")` when `schedule_at` has no timezone offset, matching the claimed behavior/message.
Fetch SQL only selects todo jobs with `scheduled_at IS NULL OR scheduled_at <= now()`, so future-scheduled jobs run at the first eligible opportunity after that time.
Croniter accepts 6-field expressions and maps seconds to field index 5, with `second_at_beginning=False` default, so seconds are in the sixth/last column unless explicitly reconfigured.
Periodic deferrer injects `task_kwargs["timestamp"] = timestamp`, where `timestamp` is the scheduled Unix epoch second for that periodic run.
`procrastinate_periodic_defers` has unique `(task_name, periodic_id, defer_timestamp)`, and each worker starts its own periodic deferrer side task (`_periodic_deferrer`).
Startup catch-up uses previous cron tick(s) but skips overdue runs beyond `max_delay`; default `MAX_DELAY` is 600s (10 minutes) and can be overridden via `App(periodic_defaults=...)`.
`procrastinate_fetch_job_v2` blocks same-lock jobs while one is `doing` and orders waiting same-lock contenders by higher priority then lower id (older first), enforcing serial lock execution.
Code confirms no lock-release NOTIFY (only insert/abort notifications) and polling fallback, but the specific ~5.6s and ~1.2s timing figures require runtime benchmarking.
Queueing lock uniqueness is enforced only for `status='todo'`; duplicate enqueue maps to `AlreadyEnqueued`, and each job has a single `lock` field so there is no multi-lock deadlock graph.

Priority default 0, descending priority selection, and 'available-jobs-only' behavior are confirmed, but the exact quoted docs warning about 'months' could not be directly verified from local source alone.
With `pass_context=True`, worker prepends `JobContext`; `JobContext` includes job, worker name/queues, `additional_context`, and `should_abort()`.
Non-abort cancel/delete applies to todo jobs, while `abort=True` marks doing jobs via `abort_requested`; worker receives aborts by DB notification and periodic abort polling (default 5s).

User-requested aborts are not effectively retried: worker suppresses retries for `JobAborted` user aborts and SQL `procrastinate_retry_job_v2` forces abort-requested running jobs to `failed` instead of `todo`.
3.9.0 source confirms middleware signature/async constraints and execution order, but 'new specifically in 3.9.0 and absent in older 3.x' needs cross-version release/source comparison.

Async abort requests cancel the asyncio job task (yielding `CancelledError` at await boundaries), while sync tasks are not preempted and must cooperatively check `context.should_abort()` and raise `JobAborted`.
